comments.author: tau-backlog
comments.text: Reproduced the memory failure locally: ~/.pi/agent/tau/memories/MEMORY.jsonl and USER.jsonl contain existing default-length nanoid ids (21 chars), while extensions/tau/src/memory/format.ts only accepted exactly 12 chars. That made parseMemoryEntries() throw `expected a nanoid`, which then broke memory.add/update/remove when those files were read. Fixed by accepting nanoid-compatible ids with length >= 12, added regression coverage for parsing legacy/default-length ids and for appending new memory to an existing file containing those ids, and verified with npm run gate in extensions/tau/.
